title: centaurea lugdunensis
intro: Centaurea lugdunensis, also known as Blue Spur or Cornflower, is a flowering plant species native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Centaurea lugdunensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in homeopathy for its medicinal properties.
